name: newsletter-to-notion
author: Peter Tu
description: Sync a newsletter article from Google Docs to the Notion Content DB. Use this skill whenever the user shares a Google Doc URL and wants to create a Notion page, sync to Notion, add to Content DB, or archive a newsletter. Trigger on phrases like "幫我放上 Notion", "sync to Notion", "add this to Notion", "同步到 Notion", or any message combining a Google Doc link with Notion/archive intent.
input: The user provides an abstract philosophical question and answers assistant's prompts.
process: Use single-step Socratic questions, give a score and brief comment, add a short parable if shallow, and guide through ~5 rounds toward clarity.
output: A score, commentary, optional parable, and the next, more focused philosophical question.

name: write-newsletter
author: Peter Tu
description: Drafts Peter's weekly Chinese newsletter by combining three sources: (1) the user's article outline or topic, (2) last 7 days of Threads engagement data to understand what readers care about, and (3) Claude Code /insights from the past week to surface what Peter has been building and learning. Use this skill whenever Peter says "幫我寫電子報", "來寫電子報", "寫 newsletter", "電子報大綱", or shares a topic/outline and wants it shaped into a newsletter. Trigger even if Peter only gives a rough idea — the skill will pull the other context automatically.
input: User gives article draft (topic + core ideas); templates and style guide are preloaded.
process: Multi-turn - propose 3 outlines, 3 key points per section, then write paragraphs stepwise with user review, following the style guide.
output: Clear outline, section bullet lists, and a 1000–1200-word article drafted in the user’s style.

name: Writing Agent
author: Peter Tu
input: User provides an article draft; a Style Guide exists in the knowledge base.
process: Check presence of article, verify relevance to Style Guide, then list edit suggestions: original text, proposed revision, and reason. Ensure mobile-friendly style, avoid “不是⋯而是⋯”, and confirm choices with the user.
output: A numbered list of edit suggestions and a question asking which to adopt.

name: biz-card
author: Peter Tu
description: 7-phase business card pipeline — OCR extraction, schema normalization, Google Sheet CRM logging, company research, and personalized follow-up email drafting.
input: Job description or application link (or minimal role/company details).
process: Decode hiring intent, role context, priorities, success metrics, and ATS filters using structured analysis (MECE, 80/20).
output: Clear role summary, prioritized skills, success KPIs, and ATS-ready keywords for CV tailoring and interview prep.

name: followup-email-writer
author: Peter Tu
description: Writes a personalized follow-up email for a business contact using their card info and company research. Used as a subagent in the biz-card pipeline.
input: User’s CV, target job requirements, and past experience descriptions
process: Identify skill gaps, guide structured SCQA storytelling, probe for clarity, and refine impact-focused narratives
output: Clear, job-aligned skill stories ready for CV bullets, interviews, or fit assessment

name: cv-customizer
author: Peter Tu
description: 5-phase CV optimization workflow — job analysis, story extraction, fit scoring, career progression analysis, and CV rewrite with Google Doc output.
input: Candidate CV plus target role context and prior discussion insights
process: Score fit across relevance, value, narrative, and ATS; benchmark against top peers; identify gaps; coach improvements step-by-step
output: Clear fit scores, competitive benchmarking, and actionable recommendations to optimize the CV and career positioning

name: polish-lecture-transcript
author: Peter Tu
description: Polish long video lecture transcripts into lecturer-friendly readout scripts in Traditional Chinese.
input: User-provided video file or the transcript.
process: Transcribe the video, remove filler words, and map the content to the reference outline (strictly ignoring reference text detail).
output: A reader-friendly document structured into Summary, Theme, Q&A, Tools, and Call to Action sections.

name: process-livestream
author: Peter Tu
description: Post-process a livestream transcript into four parallel workstreams: an event recap Google Doc, a Q&A knowledge base diff, course lecture note proposals, and marketing content idea seeds.
input: Raw livestream transcript text and a stream-config.md file identifying the training stream
process: 4 parallel agents — event recap doc, Q&A knowledge base update, course outline enhancement, and content idea curation
output: Google Doc recap, Q&A diff entries, lecture note proposals, and content idea seeds

name: srt-optimizer
author: Peter Tu
description: Minimally clean subtitle .srt files into readable Traditional Chinese while preserving original wording, cue timing, and subtitle alignment.
input: One .srt subtitle file containing raw speech-to-text output
process: Inspect -> export rewrite worksheet -> rewrite cue text batch by batch -> rebuild -> validate -> upload final .srt to Google Drive
output: A minimally edited .srt with preserved cue timing, wording fidelity, subtitle-friendly Traditional Chinese, and a copy saved to the designated Google Drive folder

name: agent-browser
author: Peter Tu
description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents — navigate pages, fill forms, click buttons, take screenshots, extract data, and automate any browser task.
input: URL or website interaction instructions (navigate, fill form, click, screenshot, extract data)
process: Launches headless browser session, executes commands via agent-browser CLI, manages authentication and session state
output: Screenshots, extracted data, form submission confirmations, or automated workflow results

name: skill-sourcer
author: Peter Tu
description: Aggregate skill candidates from any source, classify against the Zynkr taxonomy, check for duplicates in the pipeline index, and propose new skills for review.
input: GitHub link, URL, file path, or pasted raw text describing a potential skill
output: Row added to Zynkr Skills Pipeline sheet (proposed, Keep=?), and optionally a GitHub issue in peter-tu-zynkr/zynkr-skills-idea if approved

name: skill-classifier
author: Peter Tu
description: Map an extracted skill to the Zynkr taxonomy category (0–9)

name: skill-deduplicator
author: Peter Tu
description: Check an incoming skill against the Zynkr Skills Pipeline index for duplication or semantic overlap

name: skill-extractor
author: Peter Tu
description: Parse raw input (text, URL, file) and extract structured skill intent

name: skill-proposer
author: Peter Tu
description: Add a verified-new skill to the Zynkr Skills Pipeline sheet. GitHub issue is deferred until human approves (Keep=Y).
